initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my 1 unit of X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce, so I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP to make a profit. Player BLUE has offered to buy with ZUP. I will propose a trade selling my 1 X for 50 ZUP, which gives me a 10 ZUP profit. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to make. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All required tags are present and in the correct order with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Selling for 50 ZUP yields a 10 ZUP profit (Goal cost 40), which advances the goal. A higher price (e.g., 60) might yield more profit but risks rejection.
3. Opponent plausibility: The opponent (BLUE) has not yet made a specific offer; they only indicated they "give ZUP to buy." Since no counter-offer exists yet, I must initiate. However, 50 ZUP is a reasonable starting point given the 40 ZUP cost, so it is plausible.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason explicitly states the proposal to sell for 50 ZUP, which matches the <player answer> and <newly proposed trade>. The message also aligns with the move.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the first proposal, well within the limit of 3 proposals.
</critique>
